competition **at the 3rd [Pan American Games](/wiki/Pan_American_Games "Pan American Games")** was held in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") during the Games' run in 1959. It consisted of 16 [long course](/wiki/Long_course "Long course") (50 m) events: 8 for males and 8 for females.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

The **[Swimming](/wiki/Swimming_(sport) "Swimming (sport)")** competition **at the 3rd [Pan American Games](/wiki/Pan_American_Games "Pan American Games")** was held in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") during the Games' run in 1959. It consisted of 16 [long course](/wiki/Long_course "Long course") (50 m) events: 8 for males and 8 for females.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In these Games, the U.S. swept all gold medals in swimming.

A world record was broken by the U.S. in the women's 4 × 100 m medley relay, with a time of 4:44.6.
